internal_issues.description: Framing inconsistency between HTML and DOCX for WIN-056: HTML focuses on globe formula substitution (23.45° axial tilt); DOCX frames it as circular derivation (H(r) fitted to solar observations, then re-derived). These are complementary but inconsistently presented.
internal_issues.rationale_for_deferral: DOCX infrastructure issue — requires changes to build-doc-v4.js which no agent can auto-patch. The HTML argument is scientifically sound. Low priority given build-doc-v4.js is being deprecated (V5.0 replaced DOCX→PDF chain with HTML→PDF via Playwright). May be moot if build-doc-v4.js is removed from the codebase.

internal_issues.description: DOCX conflates WIN-057 and WIN-058 in build-doc-v4.js summary text.
internal_issues.rationale_for_deferral: DOCX infrastructure issue. Same as ISS-370 — may be moot given V5.0 replaced DOCX generation with HTML→PDF via Playwright. If build-doc-v4.js is removed, this issue resolves automatically.

internal_issues.description: Section numbering mismatch Part 4.5 between HTML (4.5.3=Gravity at Rim, 4.5.4=Solar Diameter) and DOCX (4.5.3=Solar Angular Diameter, 4.5.4=Tidal Pattern). Kill-shot table cross-reference broken in one format.
internal_issues.rationale_for_deferral: DOCX infrastructure issue — requires build-doc-v4.js edits. May be moot given V5.0 transition to Playwright PDF.

internal_issues.description: DOCX/PDF completely omits Sections 5.14–5.16 (Repository Infrastructure: Steering AI, Repository Is the Model, Monitoring Infrastructure). These are the review's most original contributions — the inject_ai_layer.py analysis, curve-fitting exposure, and blockchain timestamping critique.
internal_issues.rationale_for_deferral: DOCX infrastructure issue — requires adding three new sections to build-doc-v4.js. This is substantial work (~3,000 words of prose to add). However, since V5.0 now generates PDF directly from HTML (which does include these sections), any reader downloading the PDF via the site gets the complete content. The DOCX (Word) format is the incomplete one. Human should evaluate whether to (a) update build-doc-v4.js to include these sections, (b) deprecate build-doc-v4.js entirely in favor of the HTML→PDF chain, or (c) add a note to the DOCX indicating it is an older condensed version.

internal_issues.description: DOCX build-doc-v4.js hardcodes Self-Contradicted count as 11 in three locations (~lines 791, 812, 859). Actual count is now 13.
internal_issues.rationale_for_deferral: DOCX infrastructure issue. NOTE: count has increased since this issue was filed — was 12 when filed, now 13 (WIN-061 or WIN-067 added as Self-Contradicted in recent GitHub commits). Human should verify actual count via wins.json before patching. Same V5.0 deprecation consideration as ISS-422 applies.

internal_issues.description: DOCX SEC-6.2 cross-reference says 'Part 3.1' but should say 'Section 3.2'. HTML version is correct.
internal_issues.rationale_for_deferral: DOCX infrastructure issue. Same V5.0 deprecation consideration applies.

internal_issues.description: DOCX/PDF entirely missing Section 4.3 (Gaia kill-shot — one of six kill-shots). Any reader using the Word document is missing a major argument about 1.8 billion stellar parallax measurements.
internal_issues.rationale_for_deferral: DOCX infrastructure — requires a new section in build-doc-v4.js. Since V5.0, the PDF generated from HTML does include the Gaia kill-shot. The gap is only in the Word (.docx) format. Same deprecation consideration as ISS-422.

internal_issues.description: Verdict taxonomy: 'Misleading' conflates 4 distinct failure modes (data misrepresentation, duplication/redundancy, non-discrimination, circular derivation). 22 WINs under one label implies systematic dishonesty, but many are simply non-discriminating observations.
internal_issues.rationale_for_deferral: Verdict taxonomy restructuring requires human editorial decision. Options: (1) Add 'Non-Discriminating' subcategory, (2) Move non-discriminating WINs to 'Std Model Explains', (3) Keep current taxonomy with clearer explanatory text distinguishing the four sub-types within 'Misleading'. The counter-argument for keeping the current taxonomy: 'Misleading' accurately describes the *effect* (reader is misled about discriminating power) even if the specific *mechanism* varies. Subcategories would increase complexity and could themselves be disputed. Human editorial judgment required.

recommended_actions.action: DOCX DEPRECATION DECISION: Four major/moderate issues (ISS-422, ISS-427, ISS-440, ISS-414) require substantive changes to build-doc-v4.js. Since V5.0 now generates PDF from HTML via Playwright (which is complete and correct), the human should decide: (a) update build-doc-v4.js to add missing sections, OR (b) deprecate build-doc-v4.js and remove the Word download option, relying solely on the HTML→PDF chain. Option (b) resolves ISS-370, ISS-383, ISS-414, ISS-422, ISS-427, ISS-432, ISS-440 in one action.

VERDICT TAXONOMY REVIEW (ISS-442): Human editorial decision needed on whether to subcategorize 'Misleading' into Non-Discriminating vs. actively-misleading WINs. Currently 22 WINs under one label. Counter-argument: 'Misleading' captures the reader effect regardless of mechanism. No change may be correct.

CURMUDGEON CYCLE 2 COMPLETION: WIN-057 through WIN-067 (11 WINs) still pending Cycle 2 review. All are in the geomagnetic/cosmological clusters. No action needed — curmudgeon will pick these up on its next runs. Estimated completion: ~11 more curmudgeon runs at current pace.
